headline_summary: Strait of Hormuz shipping disruption persists, with severe traffic reduction/backlogs and reports of limited, coordinated transits (Reuters, Mar 29; Reuters, Mar 5)
Oil extends gains on conflict and chokepoint risk; market pricing reflects elevated Middle East supply/shipping risk premia (Reuters, Mar 29-30)
Pakistan hosts Turkey/Egypt/Saudi talks on proposals to reopen Strait of Hormuz and end Iran war (Reuters, Mar 29)
Iranian strikes/drones reported to damage industrial and infrastructure targets in the Gulf, including EGA’s Al Taweelah site and Kuwait airport radar (Reuters, Mar 28)
War-risk insurance premiums and shipping costs surge as Gulf conflict widens and Hormuz traffic is paralyzed (Reuters, Mar 6)
China-linked shipping signals stress: COSCO suspends bookings; two Chinese COSCO container ships abort attempted Hormuz exit (Reuters, Mar 4; Reuters, Mar 27)
Qatar declares force majeure on LNG exports amid Gulf conflict, highlighting global gas supply chain risk (Reuters, Mar 4)
Iran declares Strait of Hormuz closed and threatens ships attempting to transit (Reuters, Mar 2)
US banks placed on heightened alert for potential Iran-linked cyberattacks as war escalates (Reuters, Mar 3)

Sanctions/tactical policy actions continue alongside kinetic escalation: US announces additional Iran-linked sanctions; earlier 30-day waiver for Iranian-origin oil loaded at sea (Reuters, Mar 3; Reuters, Mar 20)
risk_drivers: Strait of Hormuz closure threats and sharply reduced vessel traffic
Direct attacks/incidents affecting commercial shipping and Gulf energy/logistics infrastructure
LNG supply disruption risk (Qatar force majeure) and spillover to global gas markets
Rising war-risk insurance and freight costs amplifying delivered energy prices
Escalation management uncertainty despite ongoing diplomacy (Hormuz reopening proposals)
Second-order cyber risk to financial sector amid conflict escalation

Energy: Higher crude risk premium while Hormuz traffic remains constrained; tail risk increases when closure threats are reiterated alongside tanker incidents.
LNG upside risk if Qatar’s force majeure and Gulf logistics constraints persist; knock-on effects to Asian and European spot markets.
Refining and petrochemical margins may widen on feedstock volatility; Middle East export schedules become less reliable.
FX: Oil-importer EM FX faces pressure on higher energy import bills; safe-haven demand can support USD in risk-off episodes.
Gulf FX pegs remain supported by policy frameworks but sensitivity rises if export receipts are interrupted for longer.
Rates: Energy-driven inflation risk can keep rate-cut expectations constrained and reprice breakevens.
Credit spreads can widen for airlines/shipping/energy-intensive sectors as insurance and logistics costs surge.
Equities: Risk-off rotations favor energy/defense; pressure on airlines, shipping, and industrials tied to Middle East supply chains.
Gulf equities can lag on geopolitical discount; global cyclicals sensitive to freight/insurance cost shock.

sources.title: Oil prices jump after Yemeni Houthis attack Israel, widening Iran conflict
Two India-bound LPG tankers clear Strait of Hormuz, government says
Pakistan hosts regional powers for Iran talks, with focus on Hormuz proposals
Emirates Global Aluminium reports 'significant damage' from Iranian strikes
Kuwait airport radar system significantly damaged after drone attack, authorities say
Iran declares Strait of Hormuz closed and threatens ships attempting to transit
Qatar shuts gas liquefaction and declares force majeure on LNG exports
Maritime war-risk insurance premiums surge as Gulf conflict widens and Hormuz traffic is paralyzed
US banks put on high alert for potential Iranian cyberattacks as war escalates
U.S. issues 30-day waiver allowing sale/delivery of Iranian-origin oil loaded at sea

events.title: Oil prices extend gains as conflict and chokepoint risk persist; Brent set for record monthly rise
events.implication: Energy risk premium remains elevated; a prolonged Hormuz disruption increases tail risk for crude and refined product benchmarks.

events.title: Two India-bound LPG tankers clear Strait of Hormuz amid broader shipping disruption
events.implication: Isolated transits do not resolve chokepoint risk; continued constraints can keep freight/insurance elevated and tighten delivered fuel markets.

events.title: Shipping disruption persists: traffic through Strait of Hormuz remains sharply reduced with backlogs
events.implication: Sustained disruption raises probability of supply/delivery delays for crude, refined products, and LNG, supporting higher prompt energy prices.

events.title: Pakistan hosts Turkey, Egypt and Saudi Arabia for Iran talks focused on reopening Hormuz proposals
events.implication: Diplomatic channels may reduce near-term escalation odds; credible reopening arrangements could lower shipping/energy risk premia.

events.title: Two Chinese COSCO container ships abort first Gulf exit attempt via Strait of Hormuz
events.implication: Operational inability to exit the Gulf suggests severe disruption; container/shipping supply chains face delays and higher costs.

events.title: Emirates Global Aluminium reports significant damage from Iranian strikes at Al Taweelah
events.implication: Demonstrates spillover to Gulf industrial infrastructure; increases regional risk premium and potential supply disruption beyond energy.

events.title: Drone attack significantly damages Kuwait International Airport radar system
events.implication: Raises aviation and logistics risk across the Gulf; reinforces insurance and security costs affecting travel and cargo.

events.title: COSCO Shipping suspends new bookings for Middle East routes amid conflict
events.implication: Container capacity reduction can push up freight rates and extend delivery times for goods moving to/from Gulf markets.

events.title: Iran declares Strait of Hormuz closed and threatens ships attempting to transit
events.implication: Explicit closure threats can rapidly reprice crude/LNG risk premia, elevate freight/insurance costs, and trigger broader risk-off moves.

events.title: Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz grinds to near halt; backlog of tankers and cargo vessels
events.implication: Severe transit reduction constrains exports from major producers and disrupts global supply chains; supports higher prompt oil prices.

events.title: Vessel traffic through Strait of Hormuz nearly halted; about 300 tankers stranded
events.implication: Large tanker backlog suggests prolonged disruption risk; can create regional product shortages and amplify global benchmark volatility.

events.title: Marine insurers cancel war-risk cover for Gulf transits as Iran conflict intensifies
events.implication: Insurance withdrawal or repricing can effectively restrict shipping, raising delivered energy costs and tightening supply.

events.title: Maritime war-risk insurance premiums surge (some >1000%) as Gulf conflict widens
events.implication: Higher war-risk costs feed into higher freight rates and delivered crude/LNG prices, adding inflation pressure and volatility.

events.title: Rare tanker transit to load Murban crude via Strait of Hormuz amid disruption
events.implication: Limited movements underscore constrained logistics; physical market differentials may widen and premiums rise for secure supply.

events.title: Multiple commercial vessels damaged by projectiles near Fujairah and the northern Strait of Hormuz
events.implication: Direct damage to ships increases operational and insurance risk, potentially deterring transit and tightening export flows.

events.title: Fuel tanker Athe Nova reported hit by drones and burning in Strait of Hormuz
events.implication: Tanker incidents can trigger immediate freight and oil price spikes and accelerate reductions in transit volumes.

events.title: Saudi Aramco shuts Ras Tanura refinery after drone strike, sources say
events.implication: Refinery outages can tighten product markets quickly; adds to crude risk premium given broader Gulf logistics constraints.

events.title: Drone attack hits fuel tank at Duqm port
events.implication: Attack on Gulf-adjacent logistics infrastructure underscores escalation; may increase security costs and rerouting.

events.title: Saudi Aramco attempts to reroute crude exports to Yanbu to bypass Hormuz; constraints highlighted
events.implication: Rerouting reveals limited spare export pathways; supports higher risk premia if alternative routes are capacity-constrained.

events.title: Qatar shuts gas liquefaction and declares force majeure on LNG exports
events.implication: LNG supply disruption risk raises spot gas prices and increases reliance on alternative suppliers; could pressure Asian and European gas balances.

events.title: US banks put on high alert for potential Iranian cyberattacks as war escalates
events.implication: Cyber risk can amplify market volatility and operational risk in payment/clearing systems during escalation; supports risk-off positioning.

events.title: US announces additional Iran-linked sanctions amid conflict escalation
events.implication: Sanctions can constrain trade/finance channels and keep risk premia elevated even if kinetic tempo eases.

events.title: US issues 30-day waiver allowing sale/delivery of Iranian-origin oil loaded at sea
events.implication: Policy waivers can temporarily reduce physical tightness by easing flows, but may be outweighed by shipping disruption/closure risk.

events.title: Hezbollah launches missiles and drones at Israel; Israel strikes Beirut and sends troops into south Lebanon
events.implication: Regional multi-front escalation can broaden risk-off sentiment and keep energy chokepoint concerns elevated.

events.title: Iranian missiles hit central Israel (Tel Aviv district impacts reported)
events.implication: Deepens direct Iran-Israel conflict dynamics; increases odds of retaliatory actions affecting regional infrastructure and shipping.

events.title: Turkey says NATO air defences destroyed a ballistic missile fired from Iran; debris reported in Hatay
events.implication: Conflict spillover into NATO territory increases escalation risk and could widen risk-off positioning in regional assets.

events.title: Israel orders evacuation of swathe of southern Lebanon including Tyre; strike hits building in Baalbek
events.implication: Signals expansion of operations; sustained cross-border fighting heightens regional geopolitical discount.

events.title: Beirut hotel strike kills four; Israel says it targeted Iranian commanders
events.implication: Targeted strikes on commanders can prompt retaliatory escalation and sustain risk-off sentiment.

events.title: Katyusha rockets target US embassy in Baghdad, security sources say
events.implication: Attacks on US facilities can raise risks of broader involvement and trigger increased security measures affecting regional operations.

events.title: US orders some diplomatic staff to leave Bahrain, Iraq and Jordan as Iran conflict escalates
events.implication: Diplomatic drawdowns indicate heightened security risk and can reinforce risk premia for regional assets and logistics.

events.title: US Senate rejects war-powers measure aimed at halting attacks against Iran
events.implication: Domestic political signals about conflict duration can affect risk premia; perceived sustained operations can keep oil risk elevated.

events.title: US says Iran firing fewer missiles; US strikes to expand inland
events.implication: Shifts in kinetic tempo and targeting can change probability of retaliatory moves affecting Gulf infrastructure and shipping.

events.title: Bombing of Tehran intensifies as war enters day six
events.implication: Intensified strikes increase risk of escalatory retaliation (including at sea), sustaining oil and risk-off premiums.

events.title: Reuters: US can only confirm about a third of Iran's missile arsenal destroyed
events.implication: Suggests continued strike capacity and prolonged risk of attacks, keeping elevated risk premia in energy and shipping.

events.title: Blast damages Bahamas-flagged tanker Sonangol Namibe near Khor Al Zubair port
events.implication: Incidents near export-related ports can further deter shipping and elevate war-risk costs, tightening supply chains.

events.title: Oil prices settle up about 5% on supply concerns as Iran conflict widens
events.implication: Price response reflects rising perceived supply risk; can tighten financial conditions via inflation expectations.

events.title: US lawmakers debate measures to constrain or halt US air campaign against Iran
events.implication: Legislative constraints could affect conflict duration expectations and thus oil risk premium and broader risk sentiment.

events.title: War-risk insurance premia spike for Gulf shipping amid Hormuz threats
events.implication: Rising war-risk premia increase freight costs and can deter voyages, supporting energy prices and risk-off flows.

events.title: US orders some diplomatic staff to leave Bahrain, Iraq and Jordan as Iran conflict escalates (security backdrop)
events.implication: Signals heightened threat environment and potential for attacks on US interests; can widen regional risk premia.

events.title: IRGC claims control of Strait of Hormuz; U.S. discusses potential naval escorts for tankers
events.implication: Escort discussions highlight elevated transit risk; may mitigate some flows but also signal sustained confrontation.

links.from: Iran declares Strait of Hormuz closed and threatens ships attempting to transit
links.to: Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz grinds to near halt; backlog of tankers and cargo vessels
links.why: Explicit closure threats raise perceived danger and can reduce transits, contributing to backlogs and near-halt conditions.

links.from: Fuel tanker Athe Nova reported hit by drones and burning in Strait of Hormuz
links.to: Marine insurers cancel war-risk cover for Gulf transits as Iran conflict intensifies
links.why: High-profile tanker incidents tend to drive insurers to withdraw/reprice cover, restricting shipping.

links.from: Marine insurers cancel war-risk cover for Gulf transits as Iran conflict intensifies
links.why: Insurance withdrawal/price spikes can curtail tanker availability and raise delivered crude costs, supporting oil upside risk.

links.from: Maritime war-risk insurance premiums surge (some >1000%) as Gulf conflict widens
links.why: Higher shipping/insurance costs can widen spreads for transport and energy-intensive sectors and pressure risk assets.

links.from: Shipping disruption persists: traffic through Strait of Hormuz remains sharply reduced with backlogs
links.why: A chokepoint constraint threatens export flows and boosts the crude risk premium.

links.from: Qatar shuts gas liquefaction and declares force majeure on LNG exports
links.why: Major LNG supply disruption risk can elevate global inflation/recession fears and prompt de-risking.

links.from: US banks put on high alert for potential Iranian cyberattacks as war escalates
links.why: Financial-sector cyber threats can raise systemic risk concerns and volatility, supporting a risk-off bias.

links.from: Oil prices extend gains as conflict and chokepoint risk persist; Brent set for record monthly rise
links.why: Sustained oil price increases pressure oil-importer EM current accounts and can weaken EM FX.
